channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Every Ability The Pokémon Legends: Z-A Mega Evolutions Have In Champions
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Legends: Z-A introduced just under 50 new Mega Evolutions across the RPG’s base game and the Mega Dimension DLC. Like Legends: Arceus before it, Z-A doesn’t include Abilities, or passive perks each Pokémon has that help it in battle and open up strategic possibilities. However, Pokémon Champions, the Switch/mobile battle simulator that will act as Pokémon’s new competitive platform moving forward, is coming out next week, and will bring each of these superpowered transformations to the competitive scene. As such, each of them are finally getting Abilities. Let’s run down the ones we know so far.

Mega Dragonite – Multiscale

Mega Dragonite is taking its base form’s hidden ability Multiscale. This reduces incoming damage when Dragonite is at full HP, giving it a bit more survivability when it’s first sent out into battle. 

Mega Meganium – Mega Sol

Mega Meganinum is getting a brand new ability in Mega Sol, which allows it to attack and receive the perks of the harsh sunlight weather mechanic. This means it can use moves like Solar Beam without having to charge up for one turn and without also suffering from the drawbacks of sunlight like the increased damage from fire-type attacks.

Mega Feraligatr – Dragonize

Mega Feraligator is also getting its own ability with Dragonize, which turns all normal-type attacks into dragon-type moves and gives them a 20-percent power boost. Feraligatr turns into a water/dragon-type monster when it mega evolves which means it’s already getting an attack boost when it uses dragon-type attacks, so using powerful normal-type attacks has a ton of damage potential with all these boosts.

Mega Froslass – Snow Warning

Froslass can summon a snowstorm upon Mega Evolution thanks to Snow Warning, which isn’t a new ability, but it is new to the Froslass evolution line. This changes the weather on the battlefield for five turns, which increases the defense of ice-type Pokémon.

Mega Emboar – Mold Breaker

Unlike the other two Legends Z-A starters, Mega Emboar doesn’t get its own ability, but it still gets a decent one. Mold Breaker bypasses enemy abilities that might cause an attack to miss or be nullified.

Mega Chesnaught – Bulletproof

Mega Chesnaught takes its evolutionary line’s Hidden Ability Bulletproof and makes it the primary one. This nullifies “ball and bomb moves,” which is a bit situational, but can keep it safe from some heavy hitters like Aura Sphere and Beak Blast.

Mega Delphox – Levitate

This one’s not too surprising, as Delphox now flies on a witch’s broom when it Mega Evolves. Now it’s immune to ground-type attacks, which is good because that takes one of its weaknesses off the board as a fire-type Pokémon.

Mega Greninja – Protean

Mega Greninja’s ability Protean changes its base type to that of whatever attack it just used. This means it can open itself up to different weaknesses temporarily, but if you plan your moves right, you can likely nullify incoming attacks that might do a ton of damage.

Those are the ones we know so far, with the following still unaccounted for:

  • Mega Raichu X
  • Mega Raichu Y
  • Mega Clefable
  • Mega Victreebel
  • Mega Starmie
  • Mega Skarmory
  • Mega Chimecho
  • Mega Absol Z
  • Mega Staraptor
  • Mega Garchomp Z
  • Mega Lucario Z
  • Mega Heatran
  • Mega Darkrai
  • Mega Excadrill
  • Mega Scolipede
  • Mega Scrafty
  • Mega Eelektross
  • Mega Chandelure
  • Mega Golurk
  • Mega Pyroar
  • Mega Meowstic
  • Mega Floette
  • Mega Malamar
  • Mega Barbaracle
  • Mega Dragalge
  • Mega Hawlucha
  • Mega Zygarde
  • Mega Crabominable
  • Mega Drampa
  • Mega Golisopod
  • Mega Magearna
  • Mega Zeraora
  • Mega Falinks
  • Mega Scovillain
  • Mega Glimmora
  • Mega Tatsugiri
  • Mega Baxcalibur


https://kotaku.com/pokemon-legends-z-a-mega-evolutions-abilities-champions-2000683952
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PS Plus Subscribers Get Five PS5 Games This Month, Including A Lot Of Tomb Raider
item.content:encoded:
April is here, and along with pretty flowers blooming and rainstorms raining, the start of a new month means Sony has announced what freebies PS Plus subscribers can look forward to downloading and playing next week. And this time around, Sony is technically giving away not three, not four, but five different games, including Tomb Raider Remastered and Sword Art Online Fractured Daydream. 

On April 1, PlayStation revealed April’s monthly games, which will be available to all PS Plus subscribers across all tiers. These games will be available to add to your PlayStation library permanently starting on April 7. Once added, you can install them and play them whenever you want as long as you remain an active PS Plus Essential, Extra, or Premium subscriber. Here’s the list of games and what platforms they’re available on.

  • Lords of the Fallen (2023) – PS5
  • Tomb Raider I-II-II: Remastered  – PS5, PS4
  • Sword Art Online Fractured Daydream – PS5

You don’t want to wait too long to grab these PS Plus monthly games, as they are only available to grab for free until May 4. After that, a new batch of not-yet-announced games will be made available to subscribers.

This month’s collection of PS Plus freebies contains Tomb Raider 1-3 Remastered, which, as the name clearly indicates, includes enhanced versions of Lara Croft’s first three video game adventures. Am I bitter about the fact that just a few weeks ago, I bought the bundle containing this collection of remastered Tomb Raider games, as well as Tomb Raider 4-6 remastered, because it was on sale for a good price? Maybe a little bit. Meanwhile, Souls-like action RPG Lords of the Fallen is here, but to be clear, this is the newer game that shares the same name as its 2014 predecessor. Lastly, Sword Art Online Fractured Daydream seems like a neat anime co-op RPG experience for fans of that franchise.

Don’t forget to add March’s free games to your PlayStation account library before they go away. You have until April 6 to grab PGA Tour 2K25, Monster Hunter Rise, Slime Rancher 2, and The Elder Scrolls Online



https://kotaku.com/ps-plus-subscribers-five-free-ps5-games-tomb-raider-lords-of-the-fallen-2000683943
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Ops 3 Director Finally Discusses Its ‘Impossible Easter Egg’ That’s Been Haunting Players For Nearly A Decade
item.content:encoded:
In this era of rampant data mining, it’s hard to believe that an easter egg could be so complicated to trigger that it’s remained undiscovered for over a decade. Yet, after ten long years, the “Impossible Easter Egg” in Black Ops 3‘s Zombies mode continues to elude fans, and the man who put it there seems keen to keep it that way.

The Impossible Easter Egg, as it’s been dubbed by fans, was teased by Call of Duty: Black Ops 3’s executive producer and director, Jason Blundell, during a pre-release interview. According to Blundell, the easter egg was intentionally designed to be convoluted and elaborate because he was “fed up” with people discovering the secrets in previous Black Ops titles so quickly.

Blundell sat down with content creator JCbackfire earlier in the week to discuss his work at Treyarch, and, naturally, the topic of the Impossible Easter Egg came up. While he didn’t outright reveal how to trigger it, he did potentially give us a few clues…in a fittingly cryptic way.

JASON BLUNDELL FINALLY RESPONDED 8 YEARS LATER ON THE IMPOSSIBLE EASTER EGG IN BLACK OPS 3 ZOMBIES… pic.twitter.com/0MmmhoIldS

— Jon (@MrDalekJD) March 31, 2026

“I thought about two books that I’d read. The first book is by an author called Miyamoto Musashi, it’s called The Book of Five Rings,” Blundell revealed. “And the other book I was thinking about was called Here Be Dragons, which is a physics book about wormholes, and advanced physics, and extraterrestrial life and so forth.”

“I’ve always kind of thought that the idea of ‘wouldn’t it be amazing if there were things there that we couldn’t see, that were always there?'” Blundell continued. “So how do you do an impossible easter egg that could survive forever? My goal being that it would never be found…so I worked pretty much through the night.”

Maybe I’ve just been influenced by all the bizarre Impossible Easter Egg threads I’ve read through during my research, but I can’t help but feel the specific book references are in some way a hint, and not just context that Blundell peppered in to make things sound legit. Either that or Blundell is just psyop-ing the Call of Duty community into finally reading some literature.

That being said, his final comment on the easter egg makes it seem as if Blundell truly never wants it to be uncovered, so perhaps he really isn’t hinting at anything here. “The impossible easter egg—the clue is in the title. It is impossible. Stop looking for it. If you were to get it, it would mean something to you, but everything to me…because I want it to survive after all of us are gone.”



https://kotaku.com/cod-blops-3-call-of-duty-black-ops-impossible-easter-egg-2000683922
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
